title: JSON Decoding, DTOs, Domain Types, Unknown Fields, and Schema Drift · Failure Repair Bay
summary: A preserved Go Pokedex run contains one plausible command, parsing, URL, JSON, client, cache, ownership, goroutine, cancellation, persistence, security, test, packaging, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in typed PokéAPI DTO-to-domain admission boundary and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Define narrow transport structs with exact JSON tags, nested shapes, optionality, and only fields needed by the command.
Choose unknown-field tolerance deliberately, bound the body before decoding, reject trailing JSON, and preserve decode causes.
Validate ID, name, dimensions, stats, types, abilities, URLs, and collection bounds before constructing domain values.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Pokedex Go Schema Fixtures
steps.instruction:

content.text: A debug Go team handles case 2409 during a clean cross-platform package smoke. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile bounded JSON fixtures with typed domain admission and trailing-document rejection; no user-visible meaning may rely on color or cursor motion; then change one toolchain, target, artifact, migration, rollback, or restore condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Maintain minimal valid, full, missing, wrong-type, out-of-range, trailing, extra-field, and changed-version fixtures.

steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: pokedex-go-schema-fixtures-shsa9n" that uses case 2573 to reconcile bounded JSON fixtures with typed domain admission and trailing-document rejection. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact PokéAPI, read or write host files, handle real terminal or OS signals, spawn unbounded work, or claim race, leak, load, accessibility, cross-platform, package, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, race and fuzz runs, temporary files, subprocess and signal harnesses, inspected artifacts, representative platforms, accessibility review, capacity, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

At Pokedex product engineering and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Pokedex Go Schema Fixtu…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug Go team handles case 1588 during an accessible regional field-guide session. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile bounded JSON fixtures with typed domain admission and trailing-document rejection; browser code must remain deterministic and side-effect-free; then change one toolchain, target, artifact, migration, rollback, or restore condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Pokedex product engineering and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Pokedex Go Schema Fixtures, keep Pokedex Go Schema Fixtures connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Maintain minimal valid, full, missing, wrong-type, out-of-range, trailing, extra-field, and changed-version fixtures.
